heading: The things you'd ask
before deciding.
lead: The questions patients and families bring up most often, with concise answers.

items.q: Where do the operations take place?
items.a: The clinic in Konyaaltı is a consultation and follow-up centre. Operations themselves are performed at fully-equipped contracted private hospitals across Antalya. All pre-op planning and post-op visits happen at the clinic.

items.q: How is MIDCAB different from a classic bypass?
items.a: MIDCAB avoids opening the sternum. Surgery is done through a small incision under the left breast, which means less pain, a shorter hospital stay and faster return to daily life — for patients whose anatomy and vessels make them suitable candidates.

items.q: I'm coming from abroad — how does it work?
items.a: We start with a video consultation. If you're a candidate, a date is set at one of our partner hospitals; transfer and accommodation are coordinated for you. An English- or German-speaking interpreter joins your visits, and we follow up via telemedicine after you return home.

TAVI is designed for patients with aortic stenosis who would face high risk in open surgery — typically older patients or those with comorbidities. Eligibility is decided case-by-case after echo, CT imaging and a full heart-team review.

items.q: Which insurance and languages do you accept?
items.a: We work in Turkish, English and German. Many private and international insurance plans are accepted via our partner hospitals — reach out through the form for specific cover.
